title: poa buckleyana
intro: Poa buckleyana, also known as Buckley's blue grass or blue wild rye, is a species of grass that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Poa buckleyana include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's essential oils in topical applications.
